title: Gene of Interest with STOP codon
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ej1nt","text":"You will need to define a reference (“wild-type”, “WT”) sequence for your gene of interest. This protocol describes how to obtain the open reading frame (ORF) from the human ORFeome 8.1/9.1 collection and add a stop codon. Before beginning you will need to design primers specific to your ORF of interest to enable cloning of the amplified product into pDONR223.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8v84n","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"35vmm","text":"Phusion Hot Start Flex 2X Master Mix (NEB: M0536S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"976bp","text":"1 kb Plus DNA Ladder (NEB: N3200S)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d4h89","text":"Agarose (Millipore Sigma: A9539)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2hv52","text":"Agar (Millipore Sigma: A6686)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"269qh","text":"Cell Culture Dishes (ThermoFisher Scientific: 150460)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aeu9d","text":"QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit (Qiagen: 27104)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"39ilv","text":"LB Broth (Millipore Sigma: 51208)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"baams","text":"Spectinomycin (Millipore Sigma: S0692)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cphca","text":"Gateway BP Clonase Enzyme Mix (Thermofisher Scientific: 11789013)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9no22","text":"NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli (High Efficiency; C2987I)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":22,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cu3si","text":"Qubit dsDNA Quantification Assay Kits (Q32850, Q32851)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"34qcs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Obtaining Gene of Interest Streak your gene of interest (GOI) from the human ORFeome (8.1 or 9.1) onto a solid agar petri plate (LB + 50g/ml spectinomycin for ORFs in pDONR223) Note: Some of the human ORFeome uses the pDONR221 backbone: 50g/ml kanamycin. This protocol will also need to be modified if your gene of interest. Incubate over night at 37°C Pick a single colony and inoculate into 5ml liquid LB + 50g/ml spectinomycin (or other antibiotic as appropriate) in a 14 ml round bottom culture tube Incubate over night at 37°C with shaking or rotation Use ~1ml of this over night culture to make a glycerol stock (800 μl 50% glycerol + 800 μl of culture, and store at -80°C) Miniprep the remaining ~4ml of culture (QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit) Send an aliquot of miniprep DNA for sanger sequencing with M13F and M13R standard primers Store this miniprepped DNA at -20°C to be used as a template for the “Add STOP codon portion” in Step 2.

Oligos for Adding STOP codon You will need to design the following primers before you start, they need to be specific to your ORF of interest: Forward primer: ORF_BP_ F1 5’- GGGGACAACTTTGTACAAAAAAGCAGGCTCTAACTCGCATACCTCTGATAACTGCACC *** - 3’ (Include the start codon of the ORF and ~18bp of ORF specific sequence instead of *** for a more specific PCR) Note: Replace “ORF” in the oligo name with the actual ORF name Reverse primer: ORFstop_BP_R1 5’-GGGGACAACTTTGTACAAGAAAGCTGGGTGCCTTCACACGCACCTATCGAAGTCA *** -3’ (Design this oligo to have ~18bp complementary to the 3’ end of the ORF instead of *** - This oligo will add a STOP codon to your ORF) Note: Replace “ORF” in the oligo name with the actual ORF name
Amplify ORF: Incorporating STOP Codon Perform PCR: %s %s Thermocycler setting: %s %s

Small Scale BP Transformation Transform BP reactions into chemical competent E. coli cells as follows: Thaw 1 tube of NEB 5 chemical competent E. coli cells (each contains 200μl cells) for ~6 min Add 1μl LR reaction directly to 10μl of competent cells Incubate cells on ice for 30 min. Heat shock cells for 30 sec at 42°C in a H 2 O bath. Incubate cells on ice for 5 min. For each tube, recover 11μl cells in 90μl of SOC Outgrowth Medium in an eppendorf tube under rotation at 37°C for 1 hour. Plate entire culture on petri plates (LB + 50μg/ml spectinomycin) Incubate the plates at 37°C overnight. Isolate 2 colonies from each plate and inoculate in selective media (LB + 50μg/ml spectinomycin) Incubate under rotation at 37°C overnight. Mini-prep using QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit BR Qubit to obtain concentration.

Amplicon Purification Make a 1% agarose gel, run with the 1kb+ DNA ladder Excise the PCR product, and purify this DNA using the MinElute Gel Extraction Kit Elute in a final volume of 15μL TE or ddH2O. Quantify the DNA with Qubit dsDNA BR Assay Kit
Gateway BP Reaction %s %s Mix well by vortexing briefly and incubate at 25°C overnight(leave on the bench at room temperature)
creator: Warren van Loggerenberg
